title: Cat Loud Vocalization or Yowling at Night: Causes and Care
meta_desc: Learn why your cat is yowling at night, from behavioral needs to medical concerns. Discover if it's an emergency and how to help your pet sleep.
content_html:
TL;DR: Nighttime yowling is often a behavioral or age-related issue, but you should seek immediate veterinary care if it is accompanied by signs of physical pain or distress. Recording a video of the behavior can significantly help your vet determine the underlying cause.

What causes my cat to yowl or meow loudly at night?

  • Nighttime vocalization, often referred to as 'caterwauling,' involves loud, persistent meowing or howling during the night.
  • In cats, this behavior can be a form of communication, a reaction to hormonal shifts, or a sign of physical discomfort.
  • It is particularly common in senior cats experiencing cognitive changes or in unneutered cats during mating cycles.

When is nighttime vocalization considered a veterinary emergency?

  • Urgency Level: Low. In most cases, nocturnal yowling is a behavioral issue or a chronic medical condition rather than a life-threatening emergency.
  • Seek immediate care if: The vocalization is sudden and accompanied by signs of physical pain, difficulty breathing, or an inability to urinate.

How can a video of my cat yowling help a veterinarian with triage?

  • Capturing a video allows your veterinarian to observe your cat's body language and facial expressions.
  • The recording captures the specific tone of the cry, providing vital diagnostic context.
  • This helps the vet differentiate between a cat that is simply bored, one that is disoriented due to age-related cognitive dysfunction, or a pet in physical distress.

Clinical Context (Merck Veterinary Manual)

Excessive nocturnal vocalization in cats can be a manifestation of normal but undesirable behavior, or a sign of an underlying medical or behavioral problem. Potential causes include disorientation or cognitive dysfunction in older cats, seeking attention, or reacting to external stimuli such as other animals outside.

Chapter: Behavioral

Source: The Merck Veterinary Manual, 11th Edition (Page 1571)

faqs.question: Could my senior cat have dementia?
faqs.answer: Yes, Feline Cognitive Dysfunction is a common cause of nighttime disorientation and yowling in older cats.

faqs.question: Is my cat yowling because they are hungry?
faqs.answer: Cats are crepuscular, meaning they are active at dawn and dusk. They may yowl at night to solicit food or attention if they have learned it gets a reaction.

faqs.question: Can medical issues like hyperthyroidism cause this?
faqs.answer: Yes, medical conditions such as hyperthyroidism or high blood pressure can increase activity and vocalization in cats.
